diagnosis: The strategy computes a Keltner Channel (EMA center ± ATR multiplier) and enters on a close outside the channel boundaries. Long signals fire when close > upper band with RSI_14 >= rsi_long_min, ADX_14 >= adx_min, and volume_ratio >= volume_threshold. Short signals fire when close < lower band with RSI_14 <= rsi_short_max under the same ADX and volume filters. A regime gate restricts entries to "breakout" and "trend" regimes only. The strategy manages exits via an ATR-based stop-loss anchored at the EMA center, a wide ATR take-profit (6.24× in evolved params), and a 36-hour time stop. Position strength is capped at 1.0 and scaled by the normalized distance outside the band. Critical observation: the strategy explicitly emits "short" signals (lines 92-107) which must be suppressed for Phase-3 spot long-only conversion. The long-side edge is plausible: breakouts above the upper Keltner during trending/breakout regimes with volume expansion and RSI momentum confirmation exploit a well-documented momentum continuation pattern in crypto. The weakness is that evolved atr_mult=3.404 makes the upper band very wide so true breakout signals are rare and high-volatility, creating small trade counts and high per-trade variance. The current stop_loss is anchored at ema_center (not atr_mult below entry), meaning the stop widens as the position moves into profit only if the EMA catches up — a structural risk for whipsaws on wide channels. Walk-forward reported WF_Sharpe=2.30 on 142 trades which is a small sample and warrants skepticism.
long_only_hypothesis: In crypto spot markets, prices that break convincingly above a wide ATR-scaled Keltner Channel during confirmed trending/breakout regimes — with simultaneously elevated ADX (trending market structure), RSI momentum confirmation above 50, and above-average volume (volume_ratio > threshold) — tend to exhibit short-term momentum continuation. This is the classic "volatility breakout into trend" setup: the channel acts as a squeeze-release detector, volume confirms institutional participation, and regime filtering avoids choppy sideways markets where breakouts fail. In a spot long-only context, the strategy holds the asset when these conditions align and sits in USDC otherwise. The short branch is dropped entirely: a break below the lower band is treated as "no signal" (remain in cash), allowing the strategy to benefit from BTC/ETH's long-run upward drift while selectively allocating during high-conviction upside breakouts.
market_techniques: Keltner Channel volatility breakout (ATR-based channel, trend-following variant)
ADX trend-strength filter to avoid false breakouts in ranging markets
RSI momentum confirmation gate (RSI > 50 threshold for long entries)
Volume surge confirmation (volume_ratio > threshold) to validate breakout participation
Regime-conditional trading (entry only in breakout/trend regimes, cash in ranging/mean-reverting regimes)
ATR-normalized stop-loss and take-profit for volatility-adaptive risk management
Time stop as a catch-all exit for stalled breakouts (36h)
Signal strength proportional to distance outside band for fractional position sizing
Bull Market Support Band as secondary regime filter (bmsb_bullish)
OBV divergence as volume-price confirmation signal
useful_features: trend: adx_14 — primary trend-strength gate, already in use
momentum: rsi_14 — momentum confirmation for long entries, already in use
momentum: rsi_7 — shorter RSI for more responsive momentum confirmation
volume: volume_ratio — volume surge confirmation, already in use
volume: volume_zscore — z-scored volume spike detection for more robust normalization
volume: obv_divergence — OBV vs OBV SMA divergence to confirm buying pressure behind breakout
volatility: atr_14 — channel width and stop/take-profit scaling, already in use
volatility: atr_7 — shorter ATR for detecting volatility expansion at breakout
volatility: gk_vol — Garman-Klass realized vol for regime detection and vol targeting
volatility: bb_bw_percentile — Bollinger bandwidth percentile to detect pre-breakout compression (squeeze before expansion)
volatility: bb_bandwidth — raw bandwidth to measure volatility expansion after breakout
trend: bmsb_bullish — Bull Market Support Band composite flag as macro bull-market regime filter
price/return: dist_from_high_20 — distance from 20-bar high to gauge whether breakout is near resistance
price/return: ret_zscore_20 — z-scored recent returns to filter extreme overextension before entry
price/return: roc_12 — 12-bar rate of change for momentum confirmation
microstructure: buying_pressure — close-location within bar at breakout bar as entry quality filter
microstructure: pressure_imbalance — directional pressure imbalance to confirm buying dominance
microstructure: close_location — where price closed within the bar (above 0.7 = bullish breakout bar)
regime: bb_bw_percentile — low percentile = pre-breakout squeeze condition (strong prior for breakout entries)

genome_params.name: ema_period
genome_params.note: Controls EMA center of Keltner Channel. Shorter = more reactive channel. Range 10-40 covers scalp to swing. Current evolved value 19 is in the useful middle.

genome_params.name: atr_mult
genome_params.note: Width of Keltner Channel in ATR units. Lower = more frequent but noisier signals. Upper extended to 4.5 to allow very wide channels that act as strong breakout filters. Current evolved 3.404 already at top of prior range.

genome_params.name: rsi_long_min
genome_params.note: Minimum RSI_14 required for long entry. Swept from mild momentum (45) to strong momentum (65). Short-only param rsi_short_max is dropped from genome for long-only.

genome_params.name: adx_min
genome_params.note: Minimum ADX_14 for trend strength confirmation. Low values allow weak trends, high values require strong directional structure. Current evolved 14.76 is on the permissive side.

genome_params.name: volume_threshold
genome_params.note: Minimum volume_ratio (current volume / 20-bar SMA volume) for confirmation. Widened upper bound to 3.0 to allow the genome to discover high-conviction volume spike thresholds.

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiple for stop-loss distance from entry price. Note: the current code sets stop at ema_center (not entry - atr_mult*ATR); this param should be wired to the stop in the new implementation as entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr.

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiple for take-profit above entry. Upper bound raised to 10.0 to allow very wide targets for trend-riding. Current evolved 6.24 already large; genome should explore even wider.

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Maximum hours to hold a position if neither stop nor take-profit fires. 12h = intraday, 96h = 4-day swing. Controls exposure to stalled breakouts.

genome_params.name: rsi_lookback
genome_params.note: NEW PARAM: allow genome to choose RSI period (currently hardcoded to 14). Period 7 = fast, 21 = slow momentum filter.

genome_params.name: bb_squeeze_filter
genome_params.note: NEW PARAM: if True, only enter when bb_bw_percentile < 30 in the N bars preceding breakout (pre-breakout compression filter). Bool sweep to test whether requiring prior squeeze improves quality.

genome_params.name: obv_confirm
genome_params.note: NEW PARAM: if True, require obv_divergence > 0 (OBV above its 20-bar SMA) at entry. Tests whether OBV volume-price alignment adds value to the breakout filter.

genome_params.name: bmsb_filter
genome_params.note: NEW PARAM: if True, require bmsb_bullish == True (price above both BMSB SMA and EMA) as macro bull-market regime gate. Tests whether macro filtering reduces bear-market whipsaws.

do_not_optimize: consecutive_bars_outside — with value 1 this has no effect; extending to 2+ drastically reduces trade count; fix at 1
require_momentum — always True; making it False degrades signal quality; hardcode to True
require_close_outside — always True; required for honest bar-close confirmation; hardcode to True
volume_confirmation — always True; volume confirmation is a structural risk filter; hardcode to True
require_regime — always True; regime gating is the primary drawdown protection; hardcode to True
allowed_regimes — fixing to [breakout, trend] is theoretically motivated and changing adds overfitting surface; hardcode
rsi_short_max — this is a short-side parameter, entirely irrelevant post-conversion; remove from genome

risk_management.stop: ATR-anchored stop: entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14. The current implementation anchors stop at ema_center which creates variable risk depending on how far price is from the EMA at entry. This must be fixed to a fixed ATR multiple from entry for consistent position sizing. Genome sweeps stop_loss_atr_mult in [1.0, 4.0].
risk_management.take_profit: ATR-anchored take-profit: entry_price + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14. Current evolved value 6.24x ATR is wide and allows trend-riding. Genome sweeps [2.0, 10.0]. Consider partial-exit at 3x ATR and trail remainder.
risk_management.trailing: Optional trailing stop once position is +2 ATR in profit: trail at entry_price + (current_gain - 1.5 * atr_14) to lock in partial gains. Implement as a param trail_activation_atr (bool sweep) to test whether trailing improves Sortino vs fixed TP.
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-targeted sizing via backtester sizing_method=volatility_scaled: target 15-20% annualized portfolio volatility. Position size = equity * (vol_target / realized_vol_annualized). Cap at max_position_pct=0.95 for spot. Use gk_vol or realized_vol_20 as the vol estimate. This naturally reduces size during high-volatility breakouts (which have higher failure rates) and increases size during calm trending conditions.
risk_management.max_size: max_position_pct = 0.95 for spot long (near-full allocation allowed since no leverage). In practice vol-targeting will hold it well below this in high-vol regimes. Hard cap at 0.95 to retain small USDC buffer for costs.
risk_management.cooldown: min_bars_between_trades: after a stop-loss exit, impose a cooldown of at least 2x ema_period bars (38 bars at ema_period=19) before re-entering. This prevents rapid re-entry into a failing breakout environment. Wire through risk_manager min_time_between_trades_hours = ema_period * bar_hours * 2.
risk_management.risk_off: Regime gate (require_regime=True, allowed_regimes=[breakout, trend]) is the primary risk-off filter — already implemented. Additional gates to add: (1) bmsb_bullish filter to avoid longs during macro bear markets; (2) adx_min threshold blocks ranging/choppy markets; (3) circuit-breaker via risk_manager: halt new entries if equity drawdown from peak > 20% within the rolling window; (4) if gk_vol is in the top decile of its 6-month distribution, reduce position size by 50% or skip entry (extreme vol spikes often precede liquidation cascades).

metrics: CAGR (annualized geometric return on spot equity, minimum target 20% for candidate)
Sharpe ratio (annualized, risk-free=0, minimum 0.8 for candidate, 1.0 for promotion)
Sortino ratio (downside deviation only — most relevant for long-only since upside vol is desirable)
Maximum drawdown % (peak-to-trough on equity curve, hard limit 30%)
Hit rate (% of trades profitable, expect 40-55% for trend-following breakout)
Average trade PnL net of costs (must be positive after 2x commission + slippage)
Trade count per year (minimum 30/year for statistical validity; 142 trades across full history is acceptable)
Exposure % (fraction of bars in position — low exposure = lost bull-market drift; target 30-60%)
Cost sensitivity: re-run with 2x and 3x commission/slippage to verify edge survives realistic costs
Walk-forward OOS Sharpe vs in-sample Sharpe ratio (OOS degradation < 40%)
Bootstrap Sharpe confidence interval (1000 resample with replacement, verify lower 5th percentile > 0)
Per-year Sharpe stability (compute Sharpe for each calendar year 2019-2023 pre-lockbox; reject if any year < -0.3)
Profit factor (gross wins / gross losses, target > 1.4)
Average bars held (diagnoses whether time_stop is the dominant exit — if > 60% exits are time_stop, TP/SL are too wide or too narrow relative to actual market movement)
sweep_changes: REMOVE the short signal branch entirely (lines 92-107 in keltner_breakout.py): delete the elif close < lower block; the strategy now returns None when close < lower rather than emitting a short Signal
REMOVE rsi_short_max from PARAMS and from auto_evolve.py param_space — it is a dead parameter post-conversion

FIX stop_loss anchor: replace stop_loss=ema_center with stop_loss=entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr — currently the stop-loss level is set at ema_center which is structurally wrong for consistent ATR risk; note that at entry time the signal stop is set but entry_price is only known at fill (next bar open), so set stop relative to fill_price in backtester or pass atr to signal and recalculate in the fill logic
ADD bmsb_filter param (bool): if True, require current bmsb_bullish == True before entering long; wire into strategy function after adx check
ADD obv_confirm param (bool): if True, require obv_divergence > 0 at entry bar; validates buying pressure is sustained
ADD bb_squeeze_filter param (bool): if True, require that bb_bw_percentile was below 30 at any of the prior 5 bars (squeeze-then-expand pattern); add to param_space as bool
ADD rsi_lookback param (int, 7-21): make RSI period configurable in the sweep instead of hardcoded 14; use features rsi_7 or rsi_14 based on param value or compute inline

EXTEND atr_mult sweep range to [1.5, 4.5] (current max 3.5 is below the evolved value of 3.404, meaning the evolved genome is near the boundary — suspicious, likely constrained by the prior range ceiling)
EXTEND take_profit_atr_mult sweep range to [2.0, 10.0] to allow trend-riding without hard boundary pressure
ADD time_stop_hours to genome sweep [12, 96] — currently hardcoded at 36; this is a key trade duration parameter that should be tunable

ADD trailing stop capability: add trail_activation_atr_mult param (float, 1.5-4.0, bool-gated) to implement trailing once position reaches activation threshold, converting the fixed TP into a trailing exit

SET sizing_method=volatility_scaled in RiskConfig for this strategy's genome evaluations to enable vol-targeting; add vol_target as a genome param (float 0.10-0.25) to sweep the target annualized volatility
ENFORCE lockbox: all genome sweeps must use only data before 2024-06-07; the 2024-06-07 to 2026-06-07 window is sealed OOS and must not influence any parameter selection
methodological_risks: Small trade count risk: 142 trades across ~7 years of data (2019-2026) yields ~20 trades/year. Walk-forward folds of 15 days may contain 0-3 trades each, making fold-level Sharpe estimates statistically meaningless (high variance from small N). Mitigation: require minimum 30 OOS trades aggregated across all folds before declaring a genome robust.

Stop-loss anchor bug: the current code sets stop_loss=ema_center in the Signal, which is a price level not a fixed ATR distance from entry. Since entry is filled at the NEXT bar's open (entry_lag=1), the actual risk per trade varies substantially. This creates inconsistent position sizing under vol-targeted sizing. Fix is critical before sweep.

Evolved parameter boundary pressure: atr_mult evolved to 3.404 against a prior ceiling of 3.5 and take_profit_atr_mult evolved to 6.239 against a ceiling of 8.0. Parameters near boundaries indicate the optimizer was constrained, and the true optimum may be outside the swept range. The reported WF_Sharpe=2.30 may be artificially limited by boundary effects.

Regime label leakage: the regime classifier labels (breakout, trend) must be computed strictly from past data with the same embargo applied as other features. If the regime classifier uses a rolling window that extends beyond the embargo boundary, regime labels on the first val/test bars are contaminated. Verify regime computation lookback <= embargo_bars (120).

Volume filter survivorship: volume_threshold=1.98 combined with adx_min=14.76 and wide atr_mult=3.404 may produce so few qualifying bars that the backtest is dominated by a handful of extreme events (COVID crash recovery, 2021 bull run peaks, 2024 ETF-driven rallies). This creates extreme regime concentration risk — the strategy may only work during specific structural bull phases.

Forward-looking feature contamination: the strategy computes EMA inline at each bar using closes.ewm(span=ema_period).mean().iloc[-1] on a slice from bar_idx-ema_period to bar_idx+1. This is correct and leak-free. However, atr_14 and adx_14 are pulled from pre-computed feature columns — verify that build_all_features computes these without future leakage (confirmed in features.py: rolling windows use min_periods, no shift(-1) issues).

Time stop dominance: if the 36-hour time stop is the dominant exit (> 50% of trades), it signals that the TP at 6.24x ATR is rarely reached. This means the strategy effectively has an asymmetric loss function: stops at ~2.6x ATR down vs time-exits at EV near 0. Must diagnose exit reason distribution before declaring the TP parameter meaningful.

Crypto-specific structural break risk: evolved parameters from 2019-2026 span multiple structural regimes (2019 accumulation, 2020-2021 leverage-driven bull, 2022 bear/deleveraging, 2023-2024 ETF/institutional, 2025-2026 current). A walk-forward that includes 2022 bear data may produce a regime-dependent edge that disappears if the macro cycle turns. The per-year Sharpe stability check is essential to detect this.

OBV and volume_ratio collinearity: adding both obv_confirm and volume_threshold creates redundant volume signals. If both are swept as genome params, the optimizer may find configurations that double-count volume evidence, making the apparent edge fragile to any change in volume data quality or exchange reporting methodology.
